commit.message:
Add project completion notification when all PRs/issues are closed

- CheckProjectCompleteAsync polls GitHub for open PRs and issues
- Fires exactly once per process lifetime via _projectCompleteNotified flag
- Only starts checking after agents have been active (has notifications)
- Only checks when all gate notifications are resolved (no open gates)
- Auto-resolved notification with green styling in dashboard
- Dispatches to notification channels (email/teams/slack stubs)

commit.message:
fix: generated spec uses keyboard.type for contenteditable, waitForReady after project select

Three issues fixed in the generated Playwright spec:
1. page.fill() doesn't work with contenteditable spans — use
   keyboard.type() with delay instead (looks more natural too)
2. After project select or new session, must wait for 'Ready' status
   before interacting — session reinitializes with new bridge
3. Exact folder name match instead of fuzzy regex

Also: removed 2 stale duplicate specs, updated prompt template with
these patterns for future generations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Render conversation threads from data.messages

When a notification carries data.messages, display a rich
conversation thread instead of the plain body text:

- Each message shows a rounded bubble, sender name and avatar
  (or initials fallback) on the first message in each group
- Consecutive messages from the same sender are visually grouped:
  no repeated avatar/name after the first in each run
- Self-messages (no sender field) are right-aligned in green;
  others are left-aligned in the card's border colour
- senderIcon may be raw base64, a data URI, or a URL — all handled
- Plain body rendering is unchanged when data.messages is absent

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(deploy): production deploys always build, previews use diff optimization

The ignore command was skipping production builds when commits only touched
engine/docs files. Production must always track latest main.

Strategy:
- VERCEL_ENV=production or branch=main → always build (exit 1)
- Preview branches → skip if no apps/web/ or packages/shared/ changes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: orion-ui
repository.description: Orion is a retro-styled, cross-platform UI framework written in C. The framework is not embedded or mobile-targeted — it is a desktop GUI framework designed for Linux, macOS, and Windows. Its central design philosophy is to faithfully reproduce the Windows API (Win32) message-based programming model in a portable, hardware-accelerated context.

commit.message:
fix: correct nameof() references in DecisionRequirementHandler (#241)

PR #228 fixed incorrect nameof() usage in RealmAccessRequirementHandler,
ResourceAccessRequirementHandler, and RptRequirementHandler, but missed
the same issue in DecisionRequirementHandler.

The handler was logging and recording metrics using
ParameterizedProtectedResourceRequirement/Handler names instead of its
own DecisionRequirement/Handler names, making logs misleading and metrics
incorrect.

Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[ci-coach] ci: run existing Gridlock Arena tests in JS and Python jobs (#9)

* ci: run existing Gridlock Arena tests in JS and Python jobs

Both Solutions/JavaScript/The-Gridlock-Arena-of-Mythos/ and
Solutions/Python/ contain self-contained test suites that were never
executed by CI — only syntax-checked. This change:

- Adds 'Run Gridlock Arena Tests' step to build-javascript job:
  runs node The-Gridlock-Arena-of-Mythos.test.js (no npm install needed)
- Adds 'Run Gridlock Arena Tests' step to build-python job:
  runs python test_gridlock_arena.py (no pip install needed)
- Fixes Python validation to use find instead of shell glob for
  consistency with the JS job and to cover future subdirectories

All tests verified passing locally before this commit.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: trigger checks

commit.message:
fix: allow short labels (<3 chars) in API lookup endpoints

The server-side validateLabel() was enforcing a 3-char minimum on all
API endpoints (hackatar, avatar, domain, profile, etc). This is a
registration rule, not a lookup rule — domains like w.hack.tez exist
via admin exceptions and should resolve normally.

commit.message:
fix: strip markdown code fences when saving generated specs

The response from Copilot wraps the spec in ```typescript ... ```
fences. The save handler now extracts the content from inside the fence
before writing to disk, so the saved .spec.ts is valid TypeScript.

feat: complete UI overhaul — app layout with folder picker and action cards

Replace the 4-step wizard with a full application interface:

UI changes:
- App bar with project pill (project name + framework)
- 3 plain-English action cards: Write Tests, Record Session,
  Fix a Failing Test — each explains what it does and when to use it
- Auto-onboarding overlay on first launch (folder picker + AI provider)
- Native Windows folder picker and file picker via WinForms dialogs
- Project summary bar shows framework, language, file counts
- No typing required — folder/file pickers handle all path input
- URL fields pre-filled from last session

Server changes:
- POST /api/browse-folder — opens native FolderBrowserDialog
- POST /api/browse-file — opens native OpenFileDialog
- GET /api/app-config — returns saved project path + real-time scan
- POST /api/app-config — persists project path and app URL
- scanProjectDirectory() — detects framework, language, file counts
- app-config.json added to .gitignore (user-specific, not committed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix(integrations): Guard invalid HA timezone

Handle null, empty, and invalid timezone strings
from the HA config endpoint.  HAClient.get_timezone
now validates the value is a non-empty string
before returning it.  RentalControlService wraps
ZoneInfo construction in a try/except to fall back
to UTC when the string is not a valid IANA zone.

commit.message:
Fix sidebar layout: position:absolute to remove from flex flow

Root cause: .stats-sidebar is a flex-direction:column container.
Even with width:0 and overflow:hidden, the browser resolves the
sidebar's intrinsic height from its column content (char-sheet,
inventory list). That height bleeds into game-body's cross-axis,
stretching game-body taller than 100vh and pushing everything
into overflow:hidden territory — invisible to the user.

Fix: position:absolute on the sidebar removes it from the flex row
entirely. It no longer contributes any size to game-body. When
closed it is invisible (width:0). When open it overlays the map
from the left at z-index:10 — the standard overlay panel pattern.
game-body already has position:relative so it serves as the
containing block.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix(integrations): Convert RC times to UTC

Rental Control timestamps from HA carry the local
timezone offset but were stored without converting
to UTC.  Naive datetimes (no tzinfo) were treated
as UTC even when HA runs in a different zone.

Add HAClient.get_timezone() to fetch the configured
HA timezone via /api/config.  In process_events(),
fetch the timezone once per poll cycle and pass it
down through _process_integration() to
process_single_event().  Aware datetimes are now
shifted to UTC with astimezone(); naive ones are
assumed to be in the HA timezone before conversion.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

feat: import hourly statistics for Energy Dashboard

Major refactor to properly support the Energy Dashboard:

- Import hourly statistics via async_add_external_statistics
  (96 x 15-min values aggregated into 24 hourly entries per day)
- Initialize cumulative sum from recorder on startup (replaces
  RestoreEntity approach — no more race conditions)

  full day data once per day, frequent polling is unnecessary)
- Add after_dependencies for recorder in manifest
- Simplify sensors: remove RestoreEntity, extra_state_attributes

Energy Dashboard setup: add external statistic
'evn_smartmeter:energy_consumption' as grid consumption source.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix layout: override screen min-height + ResizeObserver canvas sizing

Root causes:
1. #screen-game inherited .screen { min-height: 100vh } — the game-layout
   (height: 100vh) was inside a potentially-taller container, breaking the
   flex column constraints and allowing content to overflow below the fold.
   Game body appeared empty (overflow:hidden clipped it) until sidebar
   opened and forced a reflow. Fix: #screen-game { height:100vh;
   overflow:hidden; min-height:unset }.

2. CSS height:min(100%,420px) on canvas — inside a flex column whose
   height comes from cross-axis stretch, 100% resolves inconsistently
   across browsers and collapses to 0 when sidebar width changes.
   Fix: removed all CSS sizing from #map-canvas; width/height are set
   imperatively by a ResizeObserver watching .map-panel.

3. No resize handling on sidebar toggle — even if initial size was right,
   the 200px sidebar slide-in shrank the map-panel without updating the
   canvas. Fix: ResizeObserver fires on every panel size change
   (including during the CSS transition), plus an explicit redraw 270ms
   after toggle (after the 250ms transition settles).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use node:22-slim stage to pre-install Playwright Chromium

The bundled-node approach (.playwright/node/linux-x64/node) was crashing
at Docker build time with exit code 1 after 66ms. Root cause unclear but
likely a permissions or ELF issue with the unpacked NuGet binary in the
SDK container.

Replace with a dedicated playwright-browsers stage using node:22-slim
(Debian/glibc — same libc as the final aspnet:10.0 image). This ensures:
- npx playwright@1.58.0 install chromium downloads the exact browser
  revision expected by Microsoft.Playwright NuGet 1.58.0
- The binary is built for glibc (not musl), matching the runtime container
- No PowerShell, no bundled-node path hacks — just npm tooling

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add FCM data message support

- Install firebase-admin SDK
- Add device_tokens table (fcm_token PK, user_id FK)
- POST /api/device-tokens — register an FCM token for a user
- sendFcmDataMessages(userId, payload) — fans out FCM data messages
  to all registered devices for a user; auto-removes invalid tokens
- Wire FCM into DELETE /api/notifications/:id → { type: 'dismiss', notificationId }
- Wire FCM into POST /api/notifications/:id/actions → { type: 'action', notificationId, actionTaken[, actionResponse] }
- Initialise Firebase Admin via secrets.fcm.serviceAccount (gracefully
  degraded when not configured)
- Clean up device_tokens in pruneInactiveUsers and reset-confirm
- Document FCM service account config in secrets.example.json

KilnMonitor  kiln firing dashboard for Raspberry Pi kiosk

Flask+SocketIO backend polling KilnAid cloud API, kiosk-optimized
dashboard with dynamic SVG ramp chart, connection-lost overlay,
and kiosk hub for multi-app display switching.

 Result diagram
with the JSON example. The interactive context window content was
mistakenly placed here and has been moved to ContextPrimitivesView.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: correct chapter numbering across all views

Inserting AgentLoopView at position 3 shifted every subsequent view's
chapter number by +1. All views from Chapter 06 through Chapter 18 were
labelled one below their actual position.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: update Context Window Size with recent models

- Replace GPT-4o, Claude Sonnet, Gemini 1.5 Pro, Llama 3.1 70B with
  GPT-5-mini (400K), GPT-5.4 (1050K), Claude Opus 4.6 (1M), Gemini 3.1 Pro (1M)
- Make bar token label black (#111) so it reads over coloured segments

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: rebuild Context Primitives as interactive split-panel view

- Keep 'Every agent interaction is built from the same building blocks'
  intro paragraph
- Replace static cards with a two-column split layout: left shows the
  Context Window blocks grouped as Static / Dynamic / Reserved; right
  shows a summary panel that updates when a block is selected
- Each block has an (i) icon; clicking reveals What it contains,
  Why it exists, and Token cost explanations
- Add spacing between group wrappers; fix unicode escape sequences

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace PowerShell with bundled Node.js for Playwright install

PowerShell is not in Ubuntu's default apt repos and requires Microsoft's
package feed to install. Instead, use the Node.js binary that the Playwright
.NET NuGet package bundles in the publish output (.playwright/node/linux-x64/).

playwright.ps1 is just a thin wrapper around this same Node binary — this
change calls it directly and removes the PowerShell dependency entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Wire all 17 human approval gates into agent workflows

- Add WaitForGateAsync extension method for check+poll pattern
- Inject IGateCheckService into all agents, worker, and orchestrator
- ResearcherAgent: ResearchFindings gate before merge
- ProgramManagerAgent: ResearchCompleteness + PMSpecification gates
- ArchitectAgent: ArchitectureDesign gate before merge
- PrincipalEngineerAgent: EngineeringPlan, TaskAssignment, PRReviewApproval, FinalPRApproval gates
- EngineerAgentBase: PRCodeComplete (3 call sites) + ReworkExhaustion gates
- TestEngineerAgent: SourceBugEscalation, TestResults, TestScreenshots gates
- AgentSquadWorker: ProjectKickoff gate before agent spawn
- AgentSpawnManager: AgentTeamComposition gate per agent
- WorkflowStateMachine: FinalReview sync check (Review→Completion)
- Fix test DI to register IGateCheckService
- All 167 tests pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: add manual release workflow

Add workflow_dispatch release workflow that:
- Validates semver version input
- Extracts [Unreleased] notes from CHANGELOG.md
- Renames [Unreleased] to versioned heading with date
- Updates extension.yml version field
- Commits, tags, pushes, and creates GitHub Release
